todaysEvents.summary: Trump announced 5-day postponement of US strikes on Iranian power plants on Mar 23, citing 'very productive conversations'; Iran FM immediately denied any direct talks; Brent collapsed -17% to ~$100.84 (Bloomberg, CNBC). Trump's suspension expires Mar 28. (Mar 23)

IRGC struck US Fifth Fleet HQ in Bahrain and Prince Sultan airbase in Saudi Arabia in two waves on Mar 23; Saudi Patriot intercepted ballistic missile targeting Riyadh; second missile struck uninhabited area; no production impact confirmed (Al Jazeera, Wikipedia). (Mar 23)

Israel and US launched 'unprecedented' infrastructure strikes across Iran on Mar 23; Iranian Red Crescent: 80,000+ residential structures hit across Tehran, Tabriz, Isfahan, Ahvaz; Iran struck northern Israel; IDF struck Hezbollah in Beirut (Al Jazeera, Times of Israel). (Mar 23)

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Trump's 5-day pause (expires Mar 28) catalyses back-channel dialogue despite Iran's public denial of talks. Partial Hormuz reopening negotiated for non-hostile states within 2 weeks. Brent retreats toward $90-100/bbl on ceasefire signal; JKM pullback to $18-20 as Qatar Ras Laffan damage is assessed. Saudi Arabia and Iran agree a bilateral standstill. P&I cover restoration begins within 30 days. ASEAN oil-importer FX stabilises. Key risk: Iran's denial of talks suggests public posturing, not genuine engagement.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Trump's pause expires Mar 28 with only cosmetic Iranian concessions; US refrains from power plant strikes but maintains current military posture. IRGC continues low-intensity attacks on Saudi and US targets. Hormuz selective-passage framework persists; Ras Laffan restart delayed 8-12 weeks. Brent settles $95-115/bbl; JKM at $20-24/MMBtu with no Qatar restart path. ASEAN oil-importer FX (IDR, PHP) under sustained pressure; iTraxx Asia IG at 170-185bp. Arab League ceasefire framework remains rhetorical.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Trump's pause expires with no progress; US strikes Iranian power plants, cutting Iran's domestic electricity supply and triggering IRGC full escalation. Iran activates Strait of Malacca closure threat; full commercial Hormuz and Red Sea blockade; Ras Laffan permanently offline for the quarter. Oil hits $160-190/bbl; LNG spot markets seize. ASEAN recession risk surges — Thailand and Philippines face double-digit fuel inflation and sovereign credit-rating action. IRGC attacks on Bahrain and Saudi Arabia Mar 23 confirm Iran is not standing down despite Trump's pause.
